about.title: About İTA
about.subtitle: A digital atlas and encyclopedia of the Islamic historiographical tradition
about.what_text: The Atlas of Islamic Historiography (İTA) is a website and encyclopedia project that introduces the contributions to historical writing made under the dynasties that ruled across thirteen regions of the Islamic world between the 1st–15th centuries AH (7th–21st centuries CE), examined through historians, works, historiographical genres, methods, and the historical concepts and terms they produced. It is the digital, visualized form of the Civilizational Basins History & Historiography (MHTT) research programme.
about.method_text: Focusing on the historical sources, historians, and historiographical processes of each region, the work proceeds through edited volumes, workshops, and symposia. Data shaped around the articles of period specialists has been de-duplicated, merged into a single database, and matched against the TDV Encyclopedia of Islam (DİA).
about.source_mhtt: Historiography in Muslim Regions — historiographical research covering Iran, Egypt, the Indian Subcontinent, and other regions.
about.source_dia: TDV Encyclopedia of Islam — 8,500+ biographical entries, teacher-student relations, geographic data, and importance scores.
about.team_macit_affil: Kocaeli University, Faculty of Theology, Islamic History Dept.
about.team_polatoglu_affil: İnönü University, Faculty of Theology, Islamic History Dept.
about.team_erol_affil: Kırklareli University, Faculty of Arts & Sciences, History Dept.
about.pillar_kronoloji_d: A timeline built around dynasties, historical events, and historians.
about.pillar_havzalar_d: The geographic regions that form the spatial ground of Islamic historiography.
about.pillar_hanedanlar_d: Dynasties contributing to historiography politically and culturally.
about.pillar_tarihciler_d: Authors renowned in the field of history.
about.pillar_kaynaklar_d: The historical works composed by these historians.
about.pillar_turler_d: The historiographical genres that shaped how sources were written.
about.pillar_metodoloji_d: The methods and principles that govern record-keeping.
about.pillar_kavramlar_d: The concepts and terms that make historiography possible.
about.aim_text: İTA aims to present the history of Muslim historiography as a coherent whole and to frame, develop, and disseminate Islamic historiography through mapping and data visualization. By examining prominent sources and historians across thirteen regions from the early conquests to the present, it seeks to reveal an integrated picture of historical writing and to offer a bibliographic resource that lets new researchers access sources easily and compare the dynamics, perspectives, and threads across periods.
